title: Responses, Problem Details, Streaming, and Protocol-Aware Output · Go Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A production-readiness review compares near-miss decisions for responses, problem details, streaming, and protocol-aware output and requires the smallest safe single-commit response and streaming contract with changed-request evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for single-commit response and streaming contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Predict implicit 200, content sniffing, automatic length, one final status, informational responses, and header commitment order.
Serialize RFC 9457 problem type, title, status, detail, instance, extensions, and application/problem+json without leaking implementation data.
Map validation, authentication, authorization, absence, conflict, precondition, limit, timeout, cancellation, dependency, and internal failures consistently.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
